frameworks.name: mnemonic metacognition
frameworks.one_line: evaluation of one's own memory performance as a metacognitive process

frameworks.name: virtual-lesion rTMS
frameworks.one_line: inhibitory repetitive TMS used to perturb a target region causally

frameworks.name: individual-differences neuroimaging
frameworks.one_line: MRI neuro-profiles modulate susceptibility to a causal perturbation

core_question: How do TMS-induced neuromodulatory effects on precuneal meta-memory function depend on individual anatomical and functional brain profiles?
key_assumptions: precuneus is causally involved in mnemonic metacognition
BOLD signal magnitude indexes regional engagement during meta-memory
vertex serves as a valid control stimulation site
individual MRI profiles capture stable inter-subject variation
key_findings: precuneal BOLD magnitude correlates with individual meta-memory efficiency
this correlation diminishes after precuneal rTMS
higher precuneus-hippocampus resting-state connectivity predicts greater TMS susceptibility
smaller grey-matter volume in stimulated precuneus predicts greater TMS susceptibility
interpretations: precuneal role in meta-memory is causally affirmed and modulated by individual anatomy and connectivity
effects are domain-specific — absent in the perceptual task
